zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Wiliam De Somerville於1340年出生在英國蘇格蘭洛包爾林頓時，他的父親Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwath50歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Douglas35歲。他於1370年之前與Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1403年在他的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭斯可迪夏 波德斯林頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Wiliam De Somerville于1340年出生在英国苏格兰洛包爾林頓时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwath50岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Douglas35岁。他于1370年之前与Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1403年在他的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰斯可迪夏 波德斯林頓。

nameMeanings.name: Somerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Graveron-Sémerville in Eure (Normandy), from the ancient Germanic personal name Sigimar (see Siemer ) + Old French ville ‘settlement’. The surname was taken to Scotland in the 12th century by William de Somerville, a retainer of David, Earl of Huntingdon, who became King David I of Scotland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Somacháin (see Summers ) or Mag Shamradháin (see Somers ).

variants.name: Siemer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Helmuth, Otto, Theresia.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Siegmar, composed of the elements sigi ‘victory’ + mari, meri ‘famous’.

variants.name: Summerville
meanings: English, Scottish (Lanarkshire), and Irish: variant of   Somerville  . This form of the surname is particularly common in Ireland.

variants.name: Simrell
meanings: Scottish: perhaps related to the Ulster nameSumeril, a variant of Somerville .

variants.name: Summerfield
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + feld ‘field, open land’, denoting summer pasture or fields cultivated only in summer, such as Summerfield in Woodnesborough (Kent), Somerfield Court in Sellindge (Kent), and Summerfield in Marlborough (Wiltshire).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Sommerfeld  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Wiliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Wiliam De Somerville was born in 1340, in Linton, Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwath, was 50 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Douglas, was 35. He married Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat before 1370, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1403, in his hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Linton, Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Wiliam De Somerville est né en 1340, à Linton, Roxburghshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwath, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Douglas, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat avant 1370, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1403, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Linton, Scottish Borders, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat vor 1370 in Scotland Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Wiliam De Somervilleが1340年にイギリススコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアリントンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwathは50歳，母Lady Elizabeth Douglasは35歳であった。1370年以前にスコットランドでKatherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffatと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1403年に故郷で63歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドスコッティッシュ ボルダースリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Wiliam De Somerville(이)가 1340년 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르 린턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwath(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Douglas(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1370년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1403년 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 스콧이시 볼더스 린턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Wiliam De Somerville родился в 1340 г. в местности линтон, роксбургшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Somerville - Lord of Linton and Carnwath было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Douglas, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat перед 1370 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1403 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности линтон, скоттиш бордирс, Шотландия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Katherine Halliday - Heiress of Moffat antes de 1370, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
